content:
I updated my flake.lock and now it doesn't build with this warning + error:

Warning: supplying the --target argument to a nix-wrapped compiler may not work correctly - cc-wrapper is currently not designed with multi-target compilers in mind. You may want to use an un-wrapped compiler instead.
clang: error: argument unused during compilation: '-nostdlibinc' [-Werror,-Wunused-command-line-argument]

Using llvmPackages_18.clang-unwrapped also fails, since it's missing several standard library headers. Is there anything else that I can do?

Cc @Gary Guo @Fiona Behrens
It works fir 24.11 if you are in the mood for rolling back.

I'm building like so:

    llvm = pkgsDefault.llvmPackages_18;
    kernelMake = pkgsDefault.writeShellScriptBin "kernel-make" ''
      export HOST_CFLAGS="-I${pkgsDefault.elfutils}/include"
      export LIBCLANG_PATH="${pkgsDefault.llvmPackages_18.libclang.lib}/lib"
      export KBUILD_BUILD_TIMESTAMP="0"
      export KBUILD_BUILD_USER="user"
      export KBUILD_BUILD_HOST="host"

      ${pkgsDefault.gnumake}/bin/make \
        CC="${llvm.clang.cc}/bin/clang" \
          HOSTCC="${llvm.clang}/bin/clang" \
        HOSTCXX="${llvm.clang}/bin/clang++" \
        LD="${llvm.tools.lld}/bin/ld.lld" \
        AR="${llvm.bintools}/bin/ar" \
        NM="${llvm.bintools}/bin/nm" \
        STRIP="${pkgsDefault.stdenv.cc.bintools}/bin/strip" \
          OBJCOPY="${llvm.bintools}/bin/objcopy" \
        OBJDUMP="${llvm.bintools}/bin/objdump" \
        READELF="${llvm.bintools}/bin/readelf" \
        HOSTAR="${llvm.bintools}/bin/ar" \
        HOSTLD="${llvm.tools.lld}/bin/ld.lld" \
        LLVM_AS="${llvm.libllvm}/bin/llvm-as" \
        LLVM_LINK="${llvm.libllvm}/bin/llvm-link" \
        $@
    '';
do you have your flake publicly available?
No, it is sort of messy. But you can have a copy if you want.
flake and lockfile
yeah that would be very useful, thanks!
flake.tgz
I use it with direnv in the directory where I have all my kernel trees checked out.
Then I just cd my-awesome-tree; kernel-make vmlinux or whatever
You might want to get rid of my impure b4 fork in there

You hit the part about nix I hate most :)
You should be able to use wrapped clang for host, and then unwrapped clang as CC, that’s what I did back in the days with building in macOS in my blog as that has the same problem
That's what I did, I pass the unwrapped clang to CC=
I had to do it for my helper LTO series because wrapped clang adds too many arguments which isn't used for linking.
Aahh, reminded me that I wanted to update the box repo to have a new proper flake. But forgor
*nix repo
what do you mean by the "nix repo"?
There is a nix repo in the GitHub org. Said at kangrejos I would add a flake dev shell, then never did because of over engineering
ahh I see

some strange things happens when I use your config @Andreas Hindborg:

  • I can't run menuconfig it's complaining about missing ncurses (even though it's added)
  • when I build a config it always does a fully fresh rebuild
  • it disables RUST
  • I also haven't seen bindgen in your flake

Am I missing something?
Rust tools are not part of the shell. I supply them from outside env via rustup.
I only use this flake for dev work. I have another one for ci that supplies everything including rust tools.

Benno Lossin said:

  • when I build a config it always does a fully fresh rebuild

this isn't true btw, for some reason it did a rebuild once, but now it doesn't
Not sure about curses. Maybe that is also picked up from my env. I’m not running the shell pure.
in my devshell I have it in packages rather than nativeBuildInputs
and I'm also not using mkDerivation but just mkShell
(and you also have a typo for your arm64 setup, it's called amd64 :)

Benno Lossin said:

Benno Lossin said:

  • when I build a config it always does a fully fresh rebuild

this isn't true btw, for some reason it did a rebuild once, but now it doesn't

I set a few vars that will cause a rebuild if they are different than what you usually set them to. I do this to be able to build on remote hosts and sync the artefakts down without triggering a rebuild when I build locally again.

Benno Lossin said:

(and you also have a typo for your arm64 setup, it's called amd64 :)

I know, I saw it after I packed it for you :sweat_smile:

Andreas Hindborg said:

Benno Lossin said:

Benno Lossin said:

  • when I build a config it always does a fully fresh rebuild

this isn't true btw, for some reason it did a rebuild once, but now it doesn't

I set a few vars that will cause a rebuild if they are different than what you usually set them to. I do this to be able to build on remote hosts and sync the artefakts down without triggering a rebuild when I build locally again.

Host and user and such
I see, I'll just hack around it
coming here again with the exact -nostdlibinc error with my custom kernel derivation (some stuff seems to be happening in nixpkgs upstream, but not yet anything done)

Fiona Behrens said:

coming here again with the exact -nostdlibinc error with my custom kernel derivation (some stuff seems to be happening in nixpkgs upstream, but not yet anything done)

          # wrapped clang targeting arm32 says it understands
          # -fno-stack-clash-protection. bindgen does not agree. Unwrapped clang agrees with bindgen.
          cc = if kernelArch == "arm" || kernelArch == "riscv" then
            llvm.clang-unwrapped
          else
          # arm64 cannot build with unwrapped clang because it cannot find neon headers.
            llvm.clangUseLLVM;

Andreas Hindborg said:

Fiona Behrens said:

coming here again with the exact -nostdlibinc error with my custom kernel derivation (some stuff seems to be happening in nixpkgs upstream, but not yet anything done)

          # wrapped clang targeting arm32 says it understands
          # -fno-stack-clash-protection. bindgen does not agree. Unwrapped clang agrees with bindgen.
          cc = if kernelArch == "arm" || kernelArch == "riscv" then
            llvm.clang-unwrapped
          else
          # arm64 cannot build with unwrapped clang because it cannot find neon headers.
            llvm.clangUseLLVM;

this looks specific to RISC? i have a x86_64
The fall through is hit for all arch

I setup environment by wrapping make like so:

          kernelMake = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "kernel-make" ''
            #export HOST_CFLAGS="-I${pkgs.elfutils}/include"
            export LIBCLANG_PATH="${llvm.libclang.lib}/lib"
            export KBUILD_BUILD_TIMESTAMP="0"
            export KBUILD_BUILD_USER="user"
            export KBUILD_BUILD_HOST="host"

            ${pkgs.gnumake}/bin/make \
              CC="${cc}/bin/clang" \
              HOSTCC="${llvm.clang}/bin/clang" \
              HOSTCXX="${llvm.clang}/bin/clang++" \
              LD="${llvm.tools.lld}/bin/ld.lld" \
              AR="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-ar" \
              NM="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-nm" \
              STRIP="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-strip" \
              OBJCOPY="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-objcopy" \
              OBJDUMP="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-objdump" \
              READELF="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-readelf" \
              HOSTAR="${llvm.bintools-unwrapped}/bin/llvm-ar" \
              HOSTLD="${llvm.tools.lld}/bin/ld.lld" \
              BINDGEN="${bindgen}/bin/bindgen" \
              LLVM_AS="${llvm.libllvm}/bin/llvm-as" \
              LLVM_LINK="${llvm.libllvm}/bin/llvm-link" \
              ARCH="${kernelArch}" \
              RUSTFMT="${rustfmt}/bin/rustfmt" \
              $@
          '';
I'm currently evaluating to remove the generate-config.pl from nixpkgs with a work collegue (that is also maintainer of the derivation upstream)
I see. I skip that one for my testing because it is such a mess. I just provide a config externally.

yeah, in this case it's not for testing, but for my work. using a minified kernel in a nix appliance image with no modules and all that. after people from the nix community heared about it there seems to be some interest in upstreaming my work, but let's see
think it was a to old nixpkgs and somewhat did an upsie upstream. seems to be building now
